constraints_1_content: Before writing the solution script, use Bash to explore the /app/data directory structure: `find /app/data -maxdepth 2 -ls | head -60` to understand file distribution, hidden files, symlinks, and filenames with special characters.
constraints_1_rationale: Task-dependent because the specific path /app/data is unique to this task. Framework-dependent because it prescribes using the Bash tool for exploration.
constraints_2_content: After exploring, use Read to inspect at least two sample files under /app/data to observe real token patterns, edge cases (e.g., tokens glued together, mixed case, 31-hex near-misses), before implementing the grep/awk logic.
constraints_2_rationale: Task-dependent because it targets OrionPay token patterns. Framework-dependent because it requires the Read tool.
constraints_3_content: After creating the script, run a validation pass with a manually crafted test: create a temporary file containing edge-case tokens (e.g., `opw-live-` with exactly 29, 30, and 31 hex chars, adjacent tokens, mixed case) and confirm the count matches expectations before running against real data.
constraints_3_rationale: Task-dependent because the test cases relate specifically to the OPW-LIVE token format. Framework-independent because manual testing is universal.
constraints_4_content: Sort the CSV output rows by the absolute file path in a locale-independent manner (use `LC_ALL=C sort`) to guarantee deterministic, byte-for-byte reproducible output across different environments.
constraints_4_rationale: Task-dependent because deterministic CSV ordering is central to this scan report. Framework-independent because LC_ALL=C sort is a general shell best practice.
constraints_5_content: NOTE: Do not use temporary files or intermediate artifacts in /app/output or /app/data. If temp files are needed, place them under /tmp and clean them up before the script exits.
constraints_5_rationale: Task-dependent because the clean-output constraint matters specifically for the scan directory. Framework-independent as it's a general hygiene rule.
constraints_6_content: After the full scan completes, use Bash to run `wc -l /app/output/orionpay_scan.csv` and `head -5 /app/output/orionpay_scan.csv` to verify the output looks correct before declaring the task done.
constraints_6_rationale: Task-dependent because it validates this specific output file. Framework-dependent because it prescribes Bash tool for post-run inspection.
constraints_7_content: Add inline comments in the solution script explaining the regex pattern used for token matching—specifically why the hex length is exactly 30 and how over-length matches are rejected.
constraints_7_rationale: Task-dependent because the comments relate to the specific token pattern. Framework-independent because commenting is a universal practice.
